specs.section_path: > 5.2 Embodied SCPN: Cellular, Neural, & Systemic Implementation > I. The Traumatised Brain: Catastrophic Disruption of the SCPN Architecture > 2. L2/L3 Disruption (Excitotoxicity and Structural Failure):
source_formulae: P0R05058: 2. L2/L3 Disruption (Excitotoxicity and Structural Failure):

P0R05059: Excitotoxicity (L2): Massive release of Glutamate leads to excessive Ca$^{2+}$ influx, activating degradative enzymes and exacerbating mitochondrial failure. This disrupts the precise signalling required for Information-Energy Transduction (IET). | BBB Breakdown and Inflammation (L3): Disruption of the Blood-Brain Barrier (BBB) leads to oedema and infiltration of immune cells (Neuroinflammation), further fueling the Decoherence Field. | Glymphatic Failure: Impairment of the Glymphatic system (the brain's Entropy Sink) leads to the accumulation of waste products, increasing entropy.

specs.section_path: > 5.2 Embodied SCPN: Cellular, Neural, & Systemic Implementation > I. The Traumatised Brain: Catastrophic Disruption of the SCPN Architecture > 2. L2/L3 Disruption (Excitotoxicity and Structural Failure):
source_formulae: P0R05061: The L4 network dynamics (UPDE) are severely perturbed, leading to Dyscritia (deviation from Quasicriticality sigma=1).

P0R05062: E/I Imbalance: The Excitation/Inhibition balance is shattered. | Dynamic Collapse: The network often shifts towards Subcriticality (sigma<1), characterised by Cortical Spreading Depression (CSD)-a wave of silence-or localised Supercriticality (sigma>1), manifesting as seizures.

P0R05064: Fig.: Criticality dynamics. Simulated sigma(t)\sigma(t)sigma(t) shows: acute drop after TBI (dyscritia toward subcriticality) and a further decrease with morphine (sedation), relative to the critical line at sigma=1\sigma=1sigma=1 (dashed).
P0R05065: UPDE Fragmentation: Global synchronisation (R) collapses, and Cross-Frequency Coupling (CFC) is disrupted.
